title: LIFECYCLE: Remaining device name persists after deletion across app exit/reopen
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A (physically power off).
Delete Device A from the app (Settings → Remove Device → Confirm).
Verify Device B is now the only device shown.
Note Device B's displayed name (e.g., 'Sync 20').
Tap Back to exit Device List screen.
Reopen Device List screen.
Verify Device B's name still matches the noted name.
expected: Expected: Device B name remains 'Sync 20' (or whatever was noted). Actual (If Bug): Device B name incorrectly shows 'Voyager 5200' or a blank/garbled name due to stale cache not refreshed on screen re-entry.

title: LIFECYCLE: Remaining device name persists after deletion across app background/foreground
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Verify Device B is the only device shown. Note its name.
Press Home button to background the app.
Wait 10 seconds.
Reopen app from recent apps.
Verify Device B's name still matches the noted name.
expected: Expected: Device B name remains correct after app background/foreground. Actual (If Bug): Device B name shows stale/corrupted data from Device A's cache entry.

title: LIFECYCLE: Remaining device name persists after deletion across app kill/relaunch
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Verify Device B is the only device shown. Note its name.
Force-kill the app (swipe from recent apps).
Relaunch the app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify Device B's name still matches the noted name.
expected: Expected: Device B name remains correct after full app kill/relaunch. Actual (If Bug): Device B name defaults to generic name or shows Device A's name due to persistent cache corruption.

title: STATE TRIGGER: Immediate state sync after deletion — verify remaining device name without navigating away
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
IMMEDIATELY (without any navigation) observe Device B's name and connection status on the device list screen.
Note the displayed name and connection indicator color.
expected: Expected: Device B immediately shows correct name 'Sync 20' and correct connection status (green if connected, red if disconnected). Actual (If Bug): Device B shows Device A's name or incorrect connection status due to delayed/lazy cache refresh.

title: STATE TRIGGER: Immediate state sync after deletion — kill app before navigating away
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
IMMEDIATELY (within 1 second, before any UI transition completes) force-kill the app.
Relaunch the app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify Device B's name and connection status.
expected: Expected: Device B shows correct name 'Sync 20' and correct connection status. Actual (If Bug): Device B shows Device A's name or incorrect status, indicating the deletion state was not persisted before app termination.

title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Delete connected Device A while Device B is disconnected (offline)
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app. Both connected.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device B.
Turn OFF Device B (physically power off).
Swipe to Device A (still connected).
Delete Device A (Settings → Remove Device → Confirm).
Verify Device B is now the only device shown.
Verify Device B's name is 'Sync 20' (not blank/garbled).
Verify Device B's connection status shows disconnected (red/grey indicator).
Tap on Device B to access settings.
expected: Expected: Device B shows correct name 'Sync 20' and disconnected status. Settings screen for Device B opens and is functional. Actual (If Bug): Device B shows Device A's name or garbled text, connection status shows connected (green) incorrectly, or tapping Device B fails to open settings.

title: DEVICE ASYMMETRY: Delete disconnected (offline) Device A while Device B is connected
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app. Both connected.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A (physically power off).
While Device A is offline, delete Device A.
Verify Device B is now the only device shown.
Verify Device B's name is 'Sync 20'.
Verify Device B's connection status shows connected (green indicator).
Tap on Device B to access settings.
expected: Expected: Device B shows correct name 'Sync 20' and connected status. Settings screen opens and is fully functional. Actual (If Bug): Device B shows incorrect name/status or settings access is blocked/blank.

title: CONNECTION STATUS: Remaining device connection indicator remains accurate after sibling deletion
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app. Both connected.
Navigate to: Device List → Note Device B's connection indicator is green.
Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Observe Device B's connection indicator.
Disconnect Device B (turn off Bluetooth on phone or power off Device B).
Reopen Device List.
Observe Device B's connection indicator again.
expected: Expected: Device B's connection indicator shows green (connected) immediately after deletion, then changes to red/grey after disconnection. Actual (If Bug): Device B's indicator shows incorrect state (e.g., red when still connected, or stuck on green after disconnection) due to corruption from deletion operation.

title: SETTINGS ACCESS: Remaining device settings remain fully accessible after sibling deletion
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Verify Device B is the only device shown.
Tap on Device B to open its settings.
Verify all settings tabs are visible and tappable (Audio, Controls, etc.).
Navigate to: Audio Settings → Verify EQ sliders respond to touch.
Navigate to: Device Info → Verify firmware version is displayed.
Tap Back to return to Device List.
Reopen Device B settings again.
expected: Expected: Device B settings screen opens fully, all tabs functional, EQ responds, firmware version shown. Settings remain accessible on subsequent opens. Actual (If Bug): Settings screen shows blank/loading state, or tapping Device B does nothing, or settings show Device A's configuration.

title: SETTINGS ACCESS: Remaining device settings after deletion + app restart
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Force-kill the app.
Relaunch the app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Tap on Device B.
Verify settings screen loads correctly.
Navigate to: Audio Settings → Transparency Mode.
Verify all Transparency sub-settings are functional.
expected: Expected: Device B settings load correctly after app restart. Transparency Mode and all sub-settings functional. Actual (If Bug): Settings fail to load, show error, or display Device A's settings data due to stale cache.

title: MULTI-DEVICE LIST: Device list pagination/dots correct after deletion of one device
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify pagination dots show 2 devices (2 dots).
Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Observe pagination dots immediately after deletion.
Swipe left/right on the device list.
Verify only Device B is shown and no empty/blank pages exist.
expected: Expected: Pagination dots update to show 1 dot (or no dots for single device). Swiping does not reveal blank pages or Device A's ghost entry. Actual (If Bug): Pagination still shows 2 dots, or swiping reveals a blank page, or Device B appears on wrong index causing name/status mismatch.

title: MULTI-DEVICE LIST: Three-device list — delete middle device, verify remaining devices
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200), Device B (Sync 20), and Device C (Poly Studio P5) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify pagination shows 3 dots.
Swipe to Device B (middle device).
Turn OFF Device B.
Delete Device B.
Verify pagination now shows 2 dots.
Swipe to verify Device A and Device C are present and correctly named.
Verify Device A's connection status is correct.
Verify Device C's connection status is correct.
Tap on Device A → verify settings accessible.
Tap on Device C → verify settings accessible.
expected: Expected: After deleting middle device B, pagination shows 2 dots. Device A and Device C retain correct names and connection statuses. Both devices' settings are accessible. Actual (If Bug): Pagination shows wrong count, device names are swapped, or one device shows incorrect connection status due to index corruption.

title: LIFECYCLE: Remaining device state after deletion + Bluetooth reconnect cycle
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Verify Device B is shown with correct name and connected status.
Disconnect Device B (turn off Bluetooth on phone).
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect Device B (turn Bluetooth back on).
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify Device B's name and connection status are correct.
Tap Device B → verify settings accessible.
expected: Expected: Device B survives Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle with correct name and status. Settings remain accessible. Actual (If Bug): Device B shows incorrect name/status after reconnect, or settings fail to load, indicating deletion corrupted Device B's state in persistent storage.

title: LIFECYCLE: Remaining device state after deletion + phone reboot
steps: Precondition: Pair Device A (Voyager 5200) and Device B (Sync 20) to the app.
Navigate to: Device List → Swipe to Device A.
Turn OFF Device A.
Delete Device A.
Verify Device B is shown with correct name and connected status.
Reboot the phone.
Open the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device List.
Verify Device B is the only device shown.
Verify Device B's name is correct.
Verify Device B's connection status is correct.
Tap Device B → verify settings accessible.
expected: Expected: After phone reboot, Device B remains correctly displayed with proper name and connection status. Settings accessible. Actual (If Bug): Device A reappears in the list, Device B shows wrong name, or Device B is missing entirely due to cache corruption surviving reboot.
